projectDescription: An interactive educational web experience using low-poly polygon art to showcase 10 endangered Thai marine animals, exploring their biology, threats, and conservation solutions.
keyFeatures: Low-poly polygon art representations of 10 endangered Thai marine animals
Animated shard assembly/disassembly with staggered transitions
Immersive parallax scrolling ocean environment using Framer Motion
Interactive animal detail pages with intro, threat, and solution information
Horizontal scroll 'Why Polygon?' explainer scene with jumping orca animation
Depth indicator, marine snow particles, and underwater light rays
Background ambient ocean sound with toggleable controller
Responsive mobile and desktop layouts with touch support
Thai-language educational content about marine conservation
publicApiSurface: ANIMALS[] — array of polygon animal configs in src/data/animalConfig.js
animalDetails.json — structured animal content with intro/threat/solution topics
Route /landing — parallax ocean scroll scene with all animals
Route /detail?id=<animalId> — individual animal detail page with shard animation
Route /order — internal shard order editor tool
AnimalInfoBox component — floating animal information card
